prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Cold Water Flat was an American band formed in 1990 by Paul Janovitz (vocals and guitar), Ted Silva (bass) and Paul Harding (drums) when the trio studied at University of Massachusetts Amherst. Paul Janovitz is the younger brother of Bill Janovitz, lead singer and songwriter of Buffalo Tom. They began playing shows in Boston before Janovitz moved to New York; the group nearly split up, but when European label Play It Again Sam offered to release an album, they reconsidered. In 1995, they signed with MCA Records and released a self-titled second album, recorded at Fort Apache Studios, but despite getting some airplay mainly in US college radio stations the band did not reach a wider audience. Soon after the group disbanded, Janovitz went on to join Dragstrip
B. Cold Water Flat, an American musical group, was established in 1990. Its founding members were Paul Janovitz, who handled vocals and guitar, Ted Silva on bass, and Paul Harding on drums. These three individuals came together to form the band while they were students at the University of Massachusetts Amherst. Notably, Paul Janovitz is the younger sibling of Bill Janovitz, who is recognized as the lead vocalist and primary songwriter for the band Buffalo Tom. The band initially started performing live shows in the Boston area. However, their future became uncertain when Janovitz relocated to New York, leading the group to the brink of disbanding. Their plans changed when Play It Again Sam, a European record label, extended an offer to release one of their albums, prompting them to rethink their decision to split. Subsequently, in 1995, they entered into a recording contract with MCA Records. Under this new agreement, they released their second album, which was self-titled and recorded at Fort Apache Studios. Despite receiving some radio play, primarily on college radio stations across the United States, the band was unable to achieve broader public recognition. Not long after this, the group dissolved, and Janovitz subsequently joined the band Dragstrip.
C. In 1990, Paul Janovitz, Ted Silva, and Paul Harding formed Cold Water Flat, an American band that studied at the University of Massachusetts Amherst. Janovitz was the brother of Buffalo Tom's lead singer and songwriter, and they began performing in Boston before moving to New York. They approached European label Play It Again Sam and decided to split up. They signed with MCA Records in 1995 and released a self-titled second album in Fort Apache Studios, but it did not attract a larger following due to Janovitz's involvement.
